key_themes: AI and data center-driven power demand
Trump administration approval for 10 GW natural gas generation
Reaffirmed >8% long-term EPS CAGR and 2026 guidance
Premium valuation concerns with modest analyst upside
Strong utility/renewables leadership and YTD performance
summary: NextEra Energy (NEE) is experiencing positive sentiment as it capitalizes on surging electricity demand from AI/data centers through its dominant renewables portfolio and a major March 2026 approval to develop up to 10 GW of natural gas generation. The company has reaffirmed its 2026 adjusted EPS guidance ($3.92–$4.02) and long-term >8% CAGR target, supporting solid performance (≈+15% YTD, +43% 1-year as of mid-April 2026). While analysts have raised several price targets (avg ≈$95) with Overweight/Neutral ratings, concerns over rich valuation, equity issuance dilution, and expected Q1 earnings softness (report due April 23) temper near-term enthusiasm.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/NEE/)[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/NEE/)[[2]](https://newsroom.nexteraenergy.com/news-releases?l=12)
sources: 20+ news articles & company PR (Yahoo Finance, NextEra Newsroom, Motley Fool, Zacks), Analyst updates (Mizuho, BofA, Morgan Stanley, Barclays, Jefferies), 30+ X posts (earnings mentions, technical analysis, holdings)
